content:
# Impulse Hammer

**Summary**: A non-destructive method to measure rock elastic properties like reduced Young's modulus.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] applied impulse hammer measurements to obtain a continuous geomechanical profile of the reservoir rocks.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used impulse hammering via the AutoscanTM device to determine the hardness and Young's modulus of carbonate rocks.

- [[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023]] used an impulse hammer to evaluate the changes in rock hardness after acid treatment.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used impulse hammering via the AutoscanTM device to determine the hardness and Young's modulus of carbonate rocks.

- [[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023]] used an impulse hammer to evaluate the changes in rock hardness after acid treatment.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The derived reduced Young’s Modulus (E*) showed higher sensitivity to heterogeneity and anisotropy than traditional triaxial tests. ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020, p. 28]])

- Impulse hammering was used to assess the durability of the rock samples after chemical treatment. ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 42]])

- HCl reduced the initial average hardness of the sample by 70%. ([[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023, p. 45]])
